summary: Session 1 of 2: fixed the two operator-found 0.28.0 defects in the VS Code activation/wiring layer (the layer the Set 058 vscode-stub tests never exercised). (1) extension.ts activate() no longer returns early when no workspace folder is open — it registers the webview view provider AND every command unconditionally, the folder-dependent runtime (watchers, context keys, poll) stays gated and re-inits on onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders, and the onboarding auto-getStarted is gated on having a folder (workspaceState does not persist in a no-folder window). Fixes the no-folder hang + the unavailable setup/get-started commands. (2) The Get Started wizard now carries its tier selection into dabbler.setupNewProject: wizard.html posts the selected tier, WizardPanel.ts forwards executeCommand(...,{tier}), and gitScaffold.asTier() narrows the untrusted arg and skips promptTier() when valid (falls back to prompting otherwise). Fixes the double tier prompt / dead-end. Regression coverage: activationNoFolder.test.ts drives the REAL activate() with no folder and asserts the provider + bootstrap commands register, the view renders (resolveWebviewView -> non-empty HTML, no throw), and onboarding stays quiet; it disposes context.subscriptions in teardown so the 30s poll timer is cleared and mocha exits. asTier unit test added; vscode-stub gained registerWebviewViewProvider; the watcherInventory allowlist line for extension.ts was bumped 172->182 (the fix comment shifted it). Bumped 0.28.0 -> 0.28.1 and built a local .vsix for the operator's S2 UAT (vsce ls confirms all 8 dist/templates/consumer-bootstrap files ship). No release this session. TS unit 645 passing / 2 pre-existing Set-026 stub failures (untouched files); tsc clean; drift_guard exits 0.
verification_notes: gpt-5-4 cross-provider, 2 rounds (~$0.153). R1 found NO implementation defect (the early-return removal is safe — every folder-dependent call tolerates zero folders; the wizard tier handoff + asTier narrowing are correct) and raised two test-depth follow-ups: assert the view RENDERS the empty state (not just registers), and pin the no-folder onboarding behavior. Both applied: the render test resolves the captured provider against a fake WebviewView, and onboarding was made explicit + safe by gating auto-getStarted on an open folder (with a test). R2 confirmed both follow-ups closed and the onboarding-gate change non-regressive for the with-folder path = VERIFIED. Raw verdicts in s1-verification.md / -round-2.md.

reason.specifics: Session 2 (final) gates the release on the operator's live UAT of the local 0.28.1 .vsix / F5 dev host (no-folder window + wizard->tier->scaffold both tiers), then updates repository-reference.md release status, cross-provider verifies, writes change-log.md, and holds the Marketplace republish for the operator's vsix-v0.28.1 tag-push. Same orchestrator carries the fix context and the UAT checklist.
